events.title: Cookbook Author Talk: Valentine Thomas
events.description:
Born in Montreal, Valentine Thomas was trained as a lawyer in Canada and worked in finance in London for six years before completely changing her career path, becoming a world record professional spearfisherwoman and freediver and an ambassador for ocean sustainability worldwide. She is a two-time TEDx speaker, Forbes Changemaker, and book author of Good Catch: A Guide to Sustainable Fish and Seafood with Recipes from the World’s Oceans. Among her many areas of expertise, Thomas is on a mission to educate people to make good seafood choices while fostering a sense of community to create a leading change in seafood consumption. As someone who catches one fish at a time, she has a deep respect and understanding of the ocean and the need to help create a sustainable seafood system.

Limited complimentary books available, while supplies last. No registration required. Early arrival suggested. All ages welcome.

events.title: Good to Go Fair
events.description:
Pretend City Children’s Museum Good To Go Fair: A FREE Family-Friendly Event! This FREE event is perfect for families with children ages 0-5. Enjoy a variety of activities, resources, and services designed to support your child’s development and overall well-being.
TIME: 9 am-1 pm
Pretend City Parking Lot
Rain or Shine

Developmental Screenings:
⦁ Check your child’s developmental milestones and receive valuable insights into their growth
⦁ Vision Screenings: Detect early signs of vision issues to ensure your child’s eyes are healthy.
⦁ Dental Screenings: Keep your child’s smile bright and healthy with a free dental check-up.
⦁ Social-Emotional Screenings: Assess your child’s emotional well-being and get support for their mental health.
⦁ And more!
Why Attend?
Early Detection: Catch potential health concerns early, allowing for timely intervention and support.
Free Services: Access these important health screenings at no cost to you.
Expert Guidance: Receive advice and resources from trained professionals to support your child’s development and health.
PLUS, your child can earn a FREE ticket to Pretend City by completing 3 Health screenings!
Don’t miss the chance to connect with incredible organizations!
Our Sponsors: CalOptima Health, Fairmont Schools, Hoag, Orange County Department of Education, PNC Bank
*The event is free; please note admission to the museum is not included.

Brad Taylor, Lieutenant Colonel (Ret.), is a twenty-one-year veteran of the U.S. Army Infantry and Special Forces, including eight years with the 1st Special Forces Operational Detachment—Delta, popularly known as Delta Force. Taylor retired after serving more than two decades and participating in Operation Enduring Freedom and Operation Iraqi Freedom, as well as classified operations around the globe. His final military post was as assistant professor of military science at The Citadel. He is a New York Times bestselling author of eighteen Pike Logan thrillers.

Limited complimentary books available, while supplies last. Admission does not guarantee a free copy. No registration required. Early arrival suggested.

For more information, call (714) 566-3072 or email ocpl.programs@occr.ocgov.com

About the play partner: 
Founded in 1954 as Orange County’s first music organization, the Philharmonic Society of Orange County presents national and international performances of the highest quality and provides dynamic and innovative music education programs for individuals of all ages to enhance the lives of Orange County audiences through music. 
 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/philharmonicsocietyoc/  
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PhilharmonicSocietyOC  
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/PhilharmonicSoc  
Website: www.philharmonicsociety.org/
